title: SkyrimNet Plugin Manifest
description: Metadata for a plugin submission. One manifest.json per plugin directory. Cross-file constraints (title uniqueness, author matches PR author, invocation required when plugin contains actions, listing plugins have no content files) live in validate.yml — this schema is per-file structural validation only. Nearly every field is populated by the dashboard's publish form; contributors rarely author this by hand.

type.description: Plugin kind. 'bundle' = self-contained, ships prompts/triggers/actions in the plugin directory, installable directly. 'listing' = metadata-only entry pointing to an externally hosted mod (Nexus, GitHub, etc.) — requires 'external_url' and is always manually reviewed. NOTE: listing support is not yet enabled; validate.yml rejects 'listing' until v1.1.

external_url.description: Required if type == 'listing', forbidden otherwise. Must be an https:// URL. Dashboard displays the full hostname prominently — SkyrimNet does not verify or distribute the linked file.

title.description: Human-readable plugin name. ASCII printable characters only (space through ~). No leading or trailing whitespace. Must be globally unique (case-insensitive) across the repo — uniqueness enforced by validate.yml.

tagline.description: Short one-liner shown on browse cards. ASCII printable, no leading or trailing whitespace. Included in index.json.

description.description: Full description shown on the plugin detail page. Not included in index.json — fetched on-demand from manifest.json. Rendered as restricted markdown: headings (h1-h6), bold, italic, strikethrough, ordered and unordered lists, inline code, code blocks, links, blockquotes, horizontal rules. Images and raw HTML are NOT rendered (stripped or escaped by the dashboard renderer).

author.description: GitHub username. Auto-populated by the dashboard from the linked GitHub account — contributors never type it. Must match the parent directory name under plugins/ and the PR author (enforced by validate.yml). Regex matches GitHub's username rules: alphanumeric segments joined by single hyphens, 1-39 chars, no consecutive hyphens, no leading or trailing hyphens.

version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). No pre-release tags. Bump on every update. Dashboard auto-increments if not changed.

skyrimnet_version.description: Version of SkyrimNet this plugin was built and tested against, in SkyrimNet's native format (four hyphen-separated integers, e.g. '0-19-0-0' for beta 19). Auto-populated by the dashboard at publish time from the running game's version. Used for reviewer context, install-time user warnings, and future compatibility filtering.

tags.description: Short lowercase tags for filtering (e.g. 'combat', 'dialogue', 'followers'). 3-20 chars, lowercase + digits + hyphens.

nsfw.description: Must accurately reflect whether the plugin contains adult/explicit content. Understating (content is NSFW but flag is false) is an auto-reject reason. Overstating is always fine.

mods.description: Skyrim mods this plugin integrates with. Enables 'compatible with my install' filtering and mod-based search. Populated by the dashboard from the user's installed plugin list (contributors click entries from a picker, never type filenames).

name.description: Human-readable mod name (e.g. 'Apocalypse - Magic of Skyrim').

file.description: Plugin or DLL filename as it appears in the user's Data folder. Fetched directly from the installed plugin list by the dashboard — never typed by the contributor. Matched case-insensitively by the dashboard at install time. build-index.yml lowercases this value when copying into index.json.

required.description: true = plugin does not function without this mod; false = enhances/optional.

invocation.description: REQUIRED by validate.yml if the plugin contains any actions/*.yaml files. Provides reviewer context for manual action review. Never present for listing plugins or for bundles with no actions. Because community-submitted actions are always mod integrations by nature (first-party actions calling SkyrimNet's own scripts already ship with SkyrimNet and don't belong on the community hub), every action-containing plugin must declare the upstream mod(s) it depends on.

purpose.description: What the action(s) do, in plain English.

source_mods.description: Skyrim mods whose Papyrus functions the actions call. Required because community-submitted actions are always mod integrations — list each upstream mod separately so multi-mod integration packs can credit and document each dependency.

url.description: Link to the mod's page (Nexus, GitHub, etc.).

side_effects.description: Reviewer-facing explanation of what game state the action modifies and why it's safe.

attestation.description: Author's signed statement that they tested the action for at least one hour with no save corruption or quest breakage.
